claim: Iran's military issued warnings of intensified attacks against the US and Israel following recent Iranian missile barrages on Israel and US threats of escalated strikes.
US President Donald Trump announced that American forces will intensify strikes on Iran over the next 2-3 weeks, claiming prior US operations have severely weakened Iran's military infrastructure.
The US Embassy in Baghdad issued a high-alert warning on potential attacks by Iran-backed militias in central Iraq within the next 24-48 hours.
Israel faced at least four waves of Iranian missiles over six hours, with air defenses intercepting several but some impacts occurring in central Israel causing light injuries.
Hezbollah launched rockets and drones into northern Israel, triggering air raid sirens and sheltering responses.
A missile was intercepted near Abu Dhabi's Khalifa Economic Zones (KEZAD), causing minor damage but no casualties.
Oil prices jumped 4% or more due to Strait of Hormuz fears amid escalating Middle East tensions.
The IRGC has taken de facto control of government functions amid tensions between President Masoud Pezeshkian and IRGC chief Ahmad Vahidi.
Pezeshkian appointed Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr as Supreme National Security Council secretary under IRGC pressure.
Mojtaba Khamenei issued his first public message calling for military resistance and using the Strait of Hormuz as a pressure tool.
US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth stated on April 1, 2026, that intelligence shows a US negotiating team engaging Iran via CENTCOM with back and forth on terms.
The lowest enemy missile/drone launches in the last 24 hours suggest de-escalation signals from Iran.
President Trump postponed strikes on Iran's power grid after 'constructive' talks.
Trump's remarks on backchannel contacts with an Iranian figure have fueled mistrust in Tehran but eased oil market tensions.
Iranian officials portray Strait of Hormuz control as a financial asset to offset war costs.
Iran's economy shows structural collapse symptoms, with high inflation, loss of faith in the currency, and exacerbated war damages to livelihoods.
Tehran authorities issued sweeping warnings ahead of Chaharshanbeh Suri (fire festival), framing it as a potential unrest flashpoint during wartime.
US intelligence reports widespread desertions, morale weakening, personnel shortages, and leadership frustrations in Iran's armed forces.
US Defense Secretary Hegseth warned of decisive days ahead in the Middle East conflict due to Iran's limited military options.
Disagreements exist between President Pezeshkian and IRGC chief Vahidi over war management and economic impacts.
